full_text:
ONCOSYS patients

TLS examination

Samples with immune aggregates have been annotated in QuPath

In total, we have data for WES/RNA seq of 206 patients
Matilda extracted successfully the clinical data for patients from S002-S204 (first batch)
Within this batch of patients, 105 were HGSC or high-grade endometroid

Germinal centers ONLY appear in omentum tissues, and only one ovarian sample presents immune aggregates

Cohort summary

Immune aggregate presence varies across 
treatment-molecular profile groups

Germinal center presence

i_HRPs are few but they all have immune aggregate presence, opposite to what occurs in pHRD samples
The proportion of pHRP seems to be higher in the immune aggregate positive group

Low-PFS IDS HRP patients did not develop GC, but were able to establish immune aggregates

Idea

TLS Histology Atlas:
Superficial atlas on distinct tissue types
Later -> Plan a big tCycIF
Heterogeneity in TLS/GC Presence
Why do HRP PDS/IDS samples display heterogeneity in TLS and GC presence?
Can observed heterogeneity inform prognosis or response to therapies?
Patterns in Tissue Architecture
Are there distinct patterns, neighborhoods or structural changes within tissue architecture correlated with the presence of TLS?
How might these patterns influence the clinical outcomes?
Comparison of TIL and TLS Regions
How do TILs and TLS-containing samples differ spatially and functionally?
Can these differences help to classify patients or tumor behavior?
Proteomic and Transcriptomic Insights
Are there relevant proteomic or transcriptomic mechanisms in distinct areas of the tissue linked with TIL or TLS content? (sampling in GC, T cell zone, stroma, tumor)
Microenvironment/picks vs WSI scrapes
How might these mechanisms reveal therapeutic targets or biomarkers?

Extra slides

PDS HRP patients

Proportions of aggregate types annotated over HE slides

More GC have been found in i_HRD slides than i_HRP 

(those are coming from single-sample observations). 

Also, it seems like more intratumoral GC appear in HRD patients

Total cohort numbers

In total, we have data for WES/RNA seq of 206 patients
Matilda extracted successfully the clinical data for patients from S002-S204 (first batch)
Within this batch of patients, 114 were HGSC (109) or high-grade endometroid (6)

Summary table ALL

If we select omentum, and samples where we have the molecular profile information
